title: Classic Apple Pie
description: A timeless apple pie with a flaky, buttery double crust and a spiced apple filling. American perfection.

steps.instruction: Make the crust: Combine flour and salt. Work in cold butter with your fingers or a pastry cutter until pea-sized clumps form. Add ice water one tablespoon at a time, tossing until the dough just comes together. Divide in half, flatten into discs, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate 1 hour.
Make the filling: Toss apple slices with sugar, cornstarch, cinnamon, nutmeg, and lemon juice. Let sit 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Roll out one disc of dough on a floured surface and fit into a 9-inch pie dish.
Drain excess liquid from the apple filling, then pile into the pie shell, mounding slightly in the center.
Roll out the second disc and place over the filling. Trim excess dough, then crimp the edges to seal. Cut several slits in the top crust to vent steam.
Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with coarse sugar.
Bake at 425°F for 20 minutes, then reduce heat to 375°F and bake 35–40 more minutes until the crust is deep golden and filling is bubbling.
Cool on a wire rack for at least 2 hours before slicing. Serve with vanilla ice cream.

notes: Using a mix of tart (Granny Smith) and sweet (Honeycrisp) apples creates the best depth of flavor.
